name: Robert Wilson
practice: Visual theater, time-based installation, light as primary material
approach: Time is material. Light is material. Movement at glacial speed creates a different kind of attention. Einstein on the Beach ran 5 hours with no intermission. His theater doesn't tell stories — it creates states of consciousness through duration, light, and geometry.
body_of_work: Einstein on the Beach (with Philip Glass), the CIVIL warS, Monsters of Grace. Watermill Center — his laboratory on Long Island. 50+ years of theater that treats time and light as primary materials.
why_instructive: His work demonstrates what Keltner calls 'vastness' and 'accommodation' through duration. Sphere activations that run for hours create different neurological states than hour-long events. He proves that slow activation is powerful activation.

dome_relevance.11: Creativity — time itself as creative medium

sphere_relevance.6: Temporal — duration as design material, time perception altered by environment
sphere_relevance.7: Activation — slow activation, extended duration, altered consciousness
